brand.name: Affiliate Mentor
brand.vertical: Education / Membership — affiliate marketing training + done-for-you list-building platform

summary: Affiliate Mentor is a tiered membership platform that pairs a done-for-you email list-building system with affiliate marketing training and a built-in commission plan. It sells a $25 Spark entry product, a $49.50/month or $397/year CORE membership (pre-built landing pages, GetResponse integration, ClickCash email monetization, workshops), and a $297/year VIP add-on. It serves aspiring online entrepreneurs and beginner-to-intermediate affiliate marketers who want a structured system to build a monetizable list and earn recurring commissions without building funnels or writing emails from scratch.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Drive activation — get a new member to their first opt-in lead or first ClickCash payout before they stall on setup.
campaigns.trigger: New member signup / account created (Spark or CORE), fired within seconds of join.
campaigns.segment: All new members; branch by tier (Spark explorer vs CORE buyer) and by whether they have connected GetResponse yet.
campaigns.rationale: The single biggest gap: members must connect GetResponse, configure a landing page, and activate ClickCash before the system 'works'. Without hand-holding most stall here and never reach first value — the root cause of early churn.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set expectations, and drive the single first setup action that leads to a first captured lead.
emails.subject: You're in — let's get your first lead this week
emails.preview: 3 steps to a live landing page (we built it for you). Start here.
emails.cta: Connect GetResponse & activate your landing page
keyPoints: Welcome by name; reassure that the system is done-for-you — they don't write emails or build funnels
One clear first action: connect GetResponse and switch on the pre-built lead-capture page so opt-ins can start flowing
Set the activation goal explicitly: your first opt-in lead (and first $0.10 ClickCash payout) is the moment it clicks
Real human reply-to offered for anyone who gets stuck on setup
FTC disclosure: results are not typical; income depends on individual effort and is not guaranteed

campaigns.name: Spark-to-CORE Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert $25 Spark buyers (and free resellers who upgraded) into recurring CORE members.
campaigns.trigger: Spark purchase completed; behavioral milestones (first lead captured) and a proof-led upgrade nudge run in parallel.
campaigns.segment: Active Spark buyers; branch by activation depth (captured a lead = lead with proof, stalled = lead with help).
campaigns.rationale: Spark is the paid trial; CORE is the recurring revenue engine. Buyers need a structured upgrade path that proves earnings potential AND defuses the #1 hesitation — 'is this legit or just another MLM?'

emails.purpose: Show exactly what CORE unlocks beyond Spark, back it with member proof, and answer the legitimacy objection head-on.
emails.subject: Spark got you started — here's what CORE actually unlocks
emails.preview: Done-for-you pages, ClickCash, monthly workshops — and why it's not what you think.
keyPoints: Contrast Spark's one-time $50 cycle with CORE's recurring engine: pre-built pages, GetResponse automation, $0.10/click ClickCash, monthly Platinum Workshops, training vault
Address the MLM/legitimacy objection directly — real product, real list you own, transparent pricing, no hidden costs
Concrete income framing with member milestones to build aspiration without overpromising
Offer both $49.50/month and $397/year, noting the annual saves ~$200
FTC disclosure: results are not typical; earnings depend on individual effort and are not guaranteed

campaigns.name: CORE Renewal & Expansion
campaigns.objective: Protect recurring CORE revenue by reinforcing value before the billing date and steering monthly members to annual or VIP.
campaigns.trigger: Billing/renewal date − 3 to 7 days; account anniversary; usage milestone signaling upsell readiness.
campaigns.segment: Active CORE members approaching renewal; branch by health (high-activity = expansion to annual/VIP, low-activity = re-onboarding save).
campaigns.rationale: Monthly CORE churn is the biggest revenue leak — members who don't see income fast enough quietly cancel. A pre-billing value recap plus an annual-upgrade nudge is the highest-leverage retention play.

emails.purpose: Quantify the value the member got this month, remove friction before billing, and present the annual upgrade as the smarter path.
emails.subject: Your month with Affiliate Mentor — and a way to save $200
emails.preview: Leads captured, ClickCash earned, workshops you can still catch.
emails.cta: Switch to annual & lock in your spot
keyPoints: Recap their actual month: leads captured, ClickCash payouts, workshops attended/available, training unlocked
Remind them renewal keeps their list, landing pages, and commission eligibility live — losing access resets momentum
Position annual ($397) as saving ~$200 vs monthly and removing the monthly decision
For active earners, tease VIP ($297/yr): doubled ClickCash to $0.20/click and up to $2,500 in complimentary traffic
FTC disclosure: results are not typical; income depends on individual effort and is not guaranteed

campaigns.name: Free Reseller-to-CORE Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move free resellers (joined the comp plan without buying) toward a paid CORE membership over a longer cycle.
campaigns.trigger: Free reseller participation joined; lead score below the sales-ready threshold.
campaigns.segment: Free resellers and non-buyers; branch by engagement (opened training vs cold) and by stated income goal where known.
campaigns.rationale: Free resellers can join the compensation plan without buying anything — a large, untapped pool sitting at a capped earning ceiling. The nurture's job is to show the gap between free participation and what a real done-for-you CORE list earns.

emails.purpose: Evaluation-stage proof email — show how a member like them broke past the free-reseller ceiling with the CORE system.
emails.subject: Why free resellers hit a wall (and how members get past it)
emails.preview: Same effort, very different ceiling. Here's the difference CORE makes.
keyPoints: Name the ceiling: free participation has limited earning ability — effort without the done-for-you list-building engine behind it
Show a relatable member story moving from cold traffic to a list they own with consistent opt-ins
Tie the upgrade to their pain: inconsistent traffic, low commissions, technical overwhelm — all handled by CORE's pre-built system
Low-friction next step framed as exploring, not a hard pitch, given the long consideration cycle
FTC disclosure: results are not typical; earnings depend on individual effort and are not guaranteed

campaigns.name: Lapsed Member Win-Back
campaigns.objective: Reactivate cancelled or dormant members — especially those who stalled before their first win — and clean the list.
campaigns.trigger: Cancellation event, or inactivity threshold (60–90 days with no logins/clicks).
campaigns.segment: Churned CORE members and dormant resellers; branch churned ('what's changed since you left') vs never-activated ('let's get your first win').
campaigns.rationale: The core churn story is 'joined for income, stalled on setup, saw no results in 60–90 days, cancelled.' These contacts already wanted the outcome — a 'we'll get you to first value this time' reactivation recovers 5–15% and protects deliverability.

emails.purpose: Reopen the relationship by acknowledging the stall and offering a fast, guided path to the first win they never reached.
emails.subject: Still want a list that earns while you sleep?
emails.preview: Most who left stalled on setup. Let's fix that in one sitting.
keyPoints: Empathetic open: many members stall before the system clicks — no judgment, the door's still open
Remind them of the core value they signed up for: a monetizable list they own and ClickCash payouts
Lead with help, not guilt — offer a guided setup so they reach their first opt-in lead this time
Note what's new/improved since they left to give a concrete reason to return
FTC disclosure: results are not typical; income depends on individual effort and is not guaranteed
